label: Warhammer 40K
blurb: The grim darkness of the far future. Name the speaker — heresy will be noted.

quotes.hint: Battle cry, religion, and physics engine, all at once

quotes.q: Blood for the Blood God! Skulls for the Skull Throne!
quotes.hint: The chant of a very specific demographic

quotes.q: Even in death, I still serve.
quotes.hint: A sarcophagus with legs and a grudge
The reluctant hero's all-purpose catchphrase, HERO OF THE IMPERIUM

quotes.q: Knowledge is power. Guard it well.
quotes.hint: The chapter motto of Dawn of War's finest borrowers

quotes.q: An open mind is like a fortress with its gates unbarred and unguarded.
quotes.hint: The Imperium's official position on curiosity

quotes.q: Burn the heretic. Kill the mutant. Purge the unclean.
quotes.hint: The Imperium's official position on everything else
The Imperium's official position on weekends

lore.question: Complete the tagline: in the far future, there is only…
How many Chaos gods make up the pantheon?
What device sustains the Emperor's broken body?
How many Primarchs did the Emperor create?
Ork technology often works purely because of what?
lore.hint: A gestalt psychic field with a paint-job opinion: red goes fasta

lore.question: The Adeptus Mechanicus rules from which planet?
lore.hint: The red planet, now with more cog worship

lore.question: Which galaxy-splitting civil war was named for its arch-traitor?
lore.hint: Brother against brother, ten millennia of consequences

lore.question: Which fortress world famously broke before the Guard did?
lore.hint: The planet broke. The slogan survived.

lore.question: What implanted organs turn a recruit into a Space Marine?
lore.hint: Nineteen organs, one very long surgery

lore.question: What must be sacrificed daily to light the Astronomican?
lore.hint: The grimdark lighthouse runs on souls
